summary: PR #2390 decompiled and partially matched game-mode code in src/melee/gm/gm_19EF.c, adding several helper functions and expanding state/static data for the Go/Gover-related scene flow. It also changed fn_80168F2C in gm_1601.c/.h to accept an s8 parameter used by new gm_19EF.c call sites. Bot report records 5 new matches and 6 unmatched-item improvements, with one unmatched .sdata regression.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; expands the file from stubs/comments into helpers and scene setup/update code, adds includes, statics, state fields, JObj/MObj animation calls, input handling, archive loads, text rendering, and PAD_STACK use.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_19EF.c include additions and static data expansion
raw_diff: added fn_8019EFC4, fn_8019F1D0, fn_8019F2D4, fn_8019F6EC, fn_8019F810, fn_8019F9C4 excerpt
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report new matches/improvements for main/melee/gm/gm_19EF

key_files.role: Signature adjusted for fn_80168F2C so new call site can pass an s8 argument; implementation names parameter ignored.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80168F2C(void) changed to fn_80168F2C(s8 ignored)

key_files.role: Header prototype updated to match recovered/used fn_80168F2C signature.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: prototype changed from void fn_80168F2C(void) to void fn_80168F2C(s8)

key_files.role: Changed header listed by metadata, but exact diff content was not present in loaded excerpt.

what_changed.text: gm_19EF.c added many previously absent includes for related game-mode headers, item inlines, baselib controller/dobj/mobj/sislib, lb helpers, and toy headers.

gm_19EF.c replaced placeholder comments for fn_8019F9C4 and gm_801A0A10_OnEnter area with substantial C code, including static helpers fn_8019EFC4, fn_8019F1D0, fn_8019F2D4, fn_8019F6EC, fn_8019F810 and a large fn_8019F9C4 implementation excerpt.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added function bodies in gm_19EF.c

what_changed.text: Static globals and the lbl_80479A98 state struct were expanded from padding to typed fields such as HSD_Text*, HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj* arrays, counters, flags, and frame/state values.
The new code uses natural loops for repeated JObj flag updates and JObj pointer collection rather than spelling out each repeated child/object operation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 10; i > 0; i--) flag loops
raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) lbl_80479A98.x2C[i] = node loop

what_changed.text: The new code uses a switch over gm_801A4310() to select MJ_CLASSIC, MJ_ADVENTURE, or MJ_ALLSTAR handling.
fn_80168F2C was changed from no-argument to one s8 argument in both source and header, with the source parameter named ignored.
Bot report records matched-code progress of +1252 bytes and matched-data progress of +120 bytes, with 5 new matches: fn_8019F810, fn_8019F6EC, gm_801A0A10_OnEnter, fn_8019F1D0, and .bss.
Bot report records unmatched improvements for fn_8019F9C4, fn_8019F2D4, fn_8019EFC4, .data, .sbss, and .sdata2, plus one .sdata regression.

smart_moves.text: Updated the owning header and implementation together for fn_80168F2C instead of hiding the new argument behind a local declaration.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1601.c and gm_1601.h both updated

smart_moves.text: Used address-style labels and x-offset field names for unresolved globals/struct fields rather than inventing semantic names beyond what the diff proves.
Used loops and switch constructs in places where repeated object traversal/flag operations and mode dispatch are evident in the recovered source.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a newly decompiled function reveals that an existing function is called with an argument, update the real source and header signature together; if the callee does not use the value, naming it ignored documents the current evidence without inventing behavior.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_1601.h prototype changed to fn_80168F2C(s8)

decomp_lessons.lesson: For partially understood static game-mode state, typed fields and address-style/x-offset names can improve code structure while preserving uncertainty about semantics.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbl_80479A98 padding replaced with typed x0/x4/x8 fields, HSD_Text*, HSD_GObj*, and HSD_JObj* fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: Repeated child/JObj operations in UI or scene setup code are good candidates for natural counted loops when the source varies only by index or next pointer.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) stores node into lbl_80479A98.x2C
raw_diff: for (i = 10; i > 0; i--) sets or clears JObj flags

naming_conventions.text: Unresolved file statics and globals remained address-style, such as lbl_804D6698, lbl_804D66C8, and lbl_80479A98.
Partially understood struct fields used offset-like xNN names and explicit pad_NN fields rather than speculative semantic names.
The added unused parameter in fn_80168F2C was named ignored, marking the signature requirement without asserting a role for the argument.
Local object variables use short role names such as jobj, mobj, gobj, cobj, child, next, node, and trigger where the pointer role is clear.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: local declarations in fn_8019F2D4, fn_8019F6EC, fn_8019F810, fn_8019F9C4

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: PAD_STACK was used narrowly in fn_8019F810 and fn_8019F9C4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Bot report supports match/improvement progress for this PR, but loaded evidence does not isolate whether PAD_STACK was required for those specific functions.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: GET_JOBJ(gobj) was used in fn_8019F6EC before HSD_JObjReqAnimAll/HSD_JObjAnimAll.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: No review discussion is loaded explaining why GET_JOBJ was selected.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: The code uses explicit HSD_JObjReqAnimAll/HSD_JObjAnimAll and HSD_MObjReqAnim/HSD_MObjAnim sequences to drive frame/state-dependent animation matching.
A ternary expression clamps lbl_80479A98.x8 down toward lbl_80479A98.xC after calling fn_80168F2C(0).
The PR includes text-section matches and data-section movement together; bot report shows .bss matched and .data/.sbss/.sdata2 improvements, but also a .sdata regression.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This is useful intake evidence for curator review, not proof that data changes are broadly safe.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 recorded matched code 62.24% (+0.03%, +1252 bytes) and matched data 37.89% (+0.01%, +120 bytes), with 5 new matches, 6 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 unmatched .sdata regression.

follow_up_queries: fn_80168F2C s8 ignored signature call sites

human_pr_text.md: body only says Tracking PR as usual
raw_diff: truncated but includes main changed hunk excerpt
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report with match/regression summary
notes: PR body has almost no explanation, and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
raw_diff is truncated, so exact tail changes in gm_19EF.c and the gm_19EF.h one-line change are not visible in loaded evidence.
Bot report gives useful match/regression evidence, but no loaded objdiff/build log beyond the automated summary.
No reviewer intent or rationale for PAD_STACK, GET_JOBJ, data-section changes, or the fn_80168F2C signature adjustment is available beyond the diff.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Update real headers when a decompiled call site proves a signature surface
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2390 changed fn_80168F2C from void(void) to void(s8) in both gm_1601.c and gm_1601.h after gm_19EF.c introduced a call fn_80168F2C(0). The implementation names the parameter ignored, preserving uncertainty about its purpose.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use typed offset fields for partially recovered static state without overnaming
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2390 expanded lbl_80479A98 from padding into typed fields including HSD_Text*, HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj* array entries, counters, and flags, while retaining xNN/pad_NN names where semantics were not proven.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Natural loops fit repeated JObj array/flag work in scene setup code
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2390 uses counted loops for collecting ten JObj nodes and for setting/clearing flags across lbl_80479A98.x2C entries, matching the natural repeated shape of the scene UI data.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) node collection
raw_diff: for (i = 10; i > 0; i--) JObj flag updates

source_update_candidates.title: gm_19EF.c contains Go/Gover scene code using GmGover/GmGo assets
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2390 evidence shows src/melee/gm/gm_19EF.c loading assets such as GmGover.dat, GmGoCoin.dat, GmGoAnim.dat, and GmRgStnd.dat, and the pre-existing nearby comment says the file seems to handle "Go!" animations based on .dat filenames. Consider whether path facts should describe gm_19EF.c as game-mode Go/Gover scene/animation setup code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: comment says seems to handle Go animations based on .dat filenames
source_update_candidates.reason: Path classification could improve search, but exact subsystem semantics should be curator-reviewed because PR body does not explain intent.

source_update_candidates.title: Unused recovered parameters may be named ignored when signature evidence exists but role does not
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2390 changed fn_80168F2C to accept s8 ignored while the body does not use the parameter, and updated the header to match. Consider adding or cross-linking a naming/signature note that ignored is acceptable for a required but semantically unknown parameter when call/signature evidence exists.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a potential convention, not an accepted global standard from one PR alone.

source_update_candidates.title: Track data-section regressions when code-matching PRs also touch static layout
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2390 gained code and data match progress but bot report also recorded one unmatched .sdata regression. Consider whether intake/curation should flag such cases for follow-up when text progress and data movement are mixed.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: matched code +1252 bytes and matched data +120 bytes
source_update_candidates.reason: Loaded evidence supports the presence of the regression, but not whether it was accepted intentionally or later resolved.

rejection_notes: Do not infer exact semantics for lbl_80479A98 fields from xNN names alone; the diff preserves uncertainty.
Do not claim gm_19EF.c was fully matched; bot report says fn_8019F9C4, fn_8019F2D4, and fn_8019EFC4 were still unmatched improvements, not full matches.
Do not claim PAD_STACK or GET_JOBJ were required tactics; loaded evidence shows they were used, but not the specific verifier rationale.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments are loaded.
